title: XSS Impact Demonstration
module_title: Advanced XSS Exploitation and Filter Bypass
learning_objectives: Discover and classify XSS vulnerabilities across input vectors
Create impactful PoCs demonstrating business risk beyond alert boxes
scenario: A client dismisses XSS as harmless. Find XSS vulns, bypass filters, and create PoCs showing real impact beyond alert boxes.

scenario_context.setting: Web application with input filtering but incomplete XSS prevention
scenario_context.challenge: Demonstrate business impact to stakeholder who underestimates XSS severity
scenario_context.relevance: Many organizations minimize XSS risk; requires impact demonstration

subtasks.title: XSS Discovery & Classification
subtasks.description: Test all inputs. Classify: reflected, stored, DOM-based.
subtasks.detailed_guidance: Systematically test every input field (search, comments, profile, etc.). For each XSS found, classify type and document injection point.

subtasks.title: Filter Bypass Challenge
subtasks.detailed_guidance: When payloads are blocked, develop workarounds: case variation, encoding (HTML, Unicode, hex), nested tags, event handlers, protocol schemes. Document each bypass.
subtasks.evidence_required: Filter bypass log with techniques and success rates

subtasks.title: Cookie Theft PoC
subtasks.detailed_guidance: Create payload that exfiltrates session cookies to attacker-controlled server. Document attacker's ability to hijack session.
subtasks.evidence_required: Cookie theft PoC with captured credentials

subtasks.title: Advanced Impact Demo
subtasks.description: Show phishing injection, keylogging, or defacement.
subtasks.detailed_guidance: Create payload demonstrating realistic business impact: phishing form injection, keylogger implementation, or page defacement. Record PoC.
subtasks.evidence_required: Advanced impact PoC with video/screenshot evidence

subtasks.title: Client-Ready Report
subtasks.detailed_guidance: Write non-technical summary emphasizing business impact (account takeover, data theft, fraud). Include executive summary and technical appendix.
subtasks.evidence_required: Executive vulnerability report with business impact

reflection_prompt: How would you explain XSS impact to a non-technical executive? What was the most creative filter bypass you discovered?

quiz.question: Why is Stored XSS generally more dangerous than Reflected XSS?
quiz.explanation: Stored XSS persists in the application and affects all users who view the payload, making it a mass exploitation vector.

quiz.question: What is the primary security benefit of Content Security Policy (CSP)?
options: Restricts script sources and prevents inline scripts
quiz.explanation: CSP allows organizations to restrict where scripts can be loaded from, preventing inline script execution and limiting XSS impact.

portfolio_artifacts: Cookie theft PoC code and evidence
mentor_notes: Assess creativity in filter bypass and quality of impact demonstration. Executive summary should convert technical findings into business language. PoCs must be convincing but ethical.
